_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Re exploration after laparotomy / Caesarean Section
clinical_indications: Haemorrhage into intestinal canal or abdominal cavity
Peritonitis that occurs in the absence or presence of a perforation

clinical_thresholds.note: RL refers to operations performed within 60 days in association with the primary surgery.

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for planned line of treatment and advice for admission
Clinical photograph of scar from previous surgery

claim.label: Blood transfusion notes (if blood transfusion was given)

ppd_preauth.q: Detailed Clinical notes – all vitals, detailed history especially previous surgery details, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure and advice for admission?
Did clinical photograph of scar from previous surgery (optional), clinical presentation and imaging confirm the requirement of surgery?
Was the clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?
Was the history of previous surgery documented < 60 days?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and treatment details provided?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Was requirement of blood transfusion documented (if applicable)?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge submitted?

common_queries: Clinical presentation and imaging not indicative of surgery
History of previous surgery not documented within 60 days
Detailed Clinical notes missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Clinical photograph of scar from previous surgery missing or unclear at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or incomplete at claim submission
Detailed Operative Notes missing or incomplete at claim submission
Post-op USG report missing at claim submission
Blood transfusion requirement not documented (if applicable)
Discharge summary with follow-up advice not submitted

clinical_key_pointers: The term "Relaparotomy" (RL) refers to operations performed within 60 days in association with the primary surgery.

RL is categorized as early or late; radical or palliative; urgent or elective; and, planned or unplanned depending on the performed period, its purpose, urgency, and whether or not it is scheduled, respectively.
Early relaparotomy is one that is done, within 21 days of the primary surgery.
Relaparotomy is a serious complication of caesarean section.
The purpose of the relaparotomy is to relieve intestinal obstruction, maintain homeostasis, prevent infections and carryout delayed curative surgeries.
surgical_options: Drainage of blood clots and parietal hematoma
Repair of urinary bladder or bowel injuries
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY and selection of corresponding Health Benefit Package.
